name: Lindley, J. Gary
motto: "He is of a very melancholy disposition."
clubs: President of Society of M. E. '13
description: J. Gary took his freshman year at the University of Oregon. It is supposed that the climate there had a chilling effect, for he has not yet thawed out, even in our warm sunshine. A word can be heard now and then, coming from Gary—that is if you listen closely. Nevertheless he is a good student, a good friend, and a loyal supporter of U. of A.

name: Brown, Marguerite B.
motto: "A countenance in which did meet, Sweet records, promises as sweet."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of the Woman's League '09, '10, '11, '12
Secretary and Treasurer of Class of '13, '10, '11, '12
Secretary and Treasurer of Students' Ass'n, '11, '12, '13
description: "Billie" is a native daughter of the Valentine State, and proud of it. She will be missed by the U. of A. as she has been one of the most active and willing workers. Small, but as is often quoted, "In a little precious stone, what splendor lies!" Yet "Billie" always looks up to one, who is strong.

name: Elliott, Loyd C.
description: Long, lanky Elliott, teacher of prep physics and a Senior honor-man, is a man of broad experience and noble mien. He can discuss anything from religion to athletics with perfect conviction.

name: Kelly, Helena M.
motto: "And mistress of herself though China fall."
description: "Kelly" came from the land of "showers and flowers" to lend a hand in the progress of the new State. She expects to spend her future in training the young Arizonians in the high ideals of good citizenship.

name: Wilky, L. Guy
motto: "But as you know me all, a plain, blunt man."
description: Guy comes from our rival city of Phoenix, where all good farmers grow. Not caring for country life, however, he has taken an engineering course. After his graduation he expects to join the ranks of the great artists of the day—Guy is going to be a moving picture man.

name: Young, Ralph C.
motto: "From the crown of his head to the sole of his boot, he is all mirth."
description: "Brig" has rambled north, south, east and west, and has gathered sundry bits of knowledge in his wanderings. He has great confidence in his own abilities and, doubtless, will make good on leaving this institution.

name: Laura, M. Swan
motto: "She liked what e'er she saw, and her looks went everywhere."
clubs: Art and Society Editor Senior Annual
description: Laura, the Scotch lassie, is one who gives to the world the best she has, and wins the hearts of all in the doing. She has so many irons in the fire, and tends them all so well that, by her shining example, she makes life hard for her easy-going associates.

name: Ernest, L. Barnes
motto: "Strong in will, to strive, to seek, to find and not to yield."
description: "Barnsey" hailed from Prescott, establishing thereupon the famous Prescott Colony. This boy has a few characteristics which have made him a marked personage. "Bluff and the world bluffs with you, cram and you cram alone" sums up his philosophy of life.

name: H. Oliver, Coles
motto: "Nature hath framed strange fellows in her time."
description: "Ox" has been ploughing around the Campus for, Lo! these many years. He has been away once or twice to break the monotony, but finally wound up in the Class of '13. He wears glasses, looks studious, and gets by with it.

name: Lyman, La Tourrette
motto: "With a soul as strong as a mountain river"
clubs: Business Manager of the Arizona Weekly Life, '13
description: "Lottie", athirst for the conquest of the arid soil, hiked from the land of the Phoenicians, and joined the Aggies of '13. He will settle a quarter section and lay the foundation of that vast agricultural empire around Casa Grande, when he escapes the wing of his Alma Mater. He smiles,—occasionally,—and doesn't chew tobacco.

name: Cochran, Horace M.
motto: "He seems so near and yet so far!"
description: After a year's tryout at Wabash University, "Cockey" blew into Arizona and fell captive, not to the fair co-ed, but to the sunshine. He has the rare ability of getting away with the A's and doing little or nothing in the meantime. "Cockey" plays basketball to keep from getting skinny.

name: Brewer, William F.
motto: "He dwelt not with books and solitude"
description: Brewer comes from old St. Louis, all right, but claims no relationship with the brewers of that city. He passed three dull years at the Missouri School of Mines, and came west to become a man. He is reported to be a knocker and pessimist, but is famous for his broad smile and sulphurous vocabulary.

name: Schoonmaker, Hazel L.
motto: "Large of her eyes, and slim, and tall"
description: "Schoony," the girl with the ever ready smile, came here from the University of Texas. She found our old U. of A. so worth while, that she decided to stay, and spread good cheer in the Class of '13. "Schoony" has been a shining light, but where goes this beam of sunshine? Back to Texas, where her smile is always welcome.

name: Lovejoy, Arthur L.
description: Did anyone ever see "Art" when he didn't look scholarly or thoughtful? Yea! he has pondered over many mighty problems, even unto the making of our new State laws.

name: Munds, William H.
motto: "And seem a saint when most I play the devil"
description: "Billie" is one of the original three of Prescott Colony fame. Very early in the course of his University career he found that there was more to college than textbooks. Bill is a good fellow, hard-headed and autocratic, but he peddles the right kind of phosphorous and lights his own way.

name: Foster, Henry A.
motto: "As yet a young probationer and a candidate for Heaven."
description: Foster hails from the far East. He remained in Princeton for three years, but the place proved too small for him so he moved to the U. of A., where he'd have plenty of breathing space. As a mathematician he is said to be a rival of Boris Sidis of Harvard.

name: Estill, Howard
description: "Dr." Estill can always be found on the third floor of the Science Building rummaging among the bottles and bones. He has managed, however, to scrape out a University scholarship from the debris of Science. May "Doc" be successful in his chosen work.
